mnemonics: SASF: Sartaj Authored Structures Foundational. Remember SASF as the 'SAS Foundation' for data structures knowledge.
Susan Analyzed Sorting Fundamentals. Connect her name to the foundational analysis of sorting algorithms.
keyPoints: Sartaj Sahni is a renowned computer scientist and co-author of a fundamental textbook, 'Data Structures, Algorithms, and Applications in C++'.
Susan Anderson-Freed is a co-author of the same widely-used textbook, making significant contributions to its pedagogical approach.
Their work is considered a comprehensive and foundational resource for understanding the theory and practical application of data structures and algorithms.
commonMistakes: Confusing Sartaj Sahni with another author, like Mark Allen Weiss, who also writes popular data structures textbooks.
Attributing the invention of a specific data structure (e.g., AVL trees) to Sahni/Anderson-Freed instead of recognizing their role in explaining and compiling these concepts.
Overlooking the 'Applications' part of the title and focusing solely on theory, which is a key strength of their text.
